_meta.game: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest

sources.name: DKC Atlas — DKC2 Technical Documentation
sources.notes: Primary ROM hacking resource for DKC2. Physics studies section referenced but specific values not extracted in current review.

notes: DKC2 uses the same physics engine as DKC1 — 8.8 fixed-point (256 sub-pixels/pixel), signed.
DK was kidnapped — Diddy Kong and Dixie Kong are the playable pair.
DIDDY: Same physics as DKC1 Diddy. Cartwheel attack.
DIXIE: New character. Key addition: HELICOPTER SPIN (hold jump while falling = slow descent/horizontal travel).
The helicopter spin is the defining new mechanic of DKC2 — Dixie's ponytail spins to carry both Kongs.
Core physics (walk 2.0 px/f, run 3.0 px/f, jump -7 px/f, dual gravity) are assumed same as DKC1. VERIFY.
DKC2 is generally considered the best game in the DKC trilogy — level design builds extensively on the helicopter spin.

subpixel_system.description: Same as DKC1. Inherited from same physics engine.

ground_movement._note: Assumed same as DKC1. VERIFY against DKC2 ROM data.

walk_speed.notes: Assumed same as DKC1. VERIFY DKC2 ROM.

run_speed.notes: Assumed same as DKC1. VERIFY DKC2 ROM.

initial_y_velocity.notes: Assumed same as DKC1. VERIFY DKC2 ROM.

diddy_kong.notes: Diddy's base physics same as DKC1.

cartwheel.description: Horizontal rolling attack that covers distance and defeats most standard enemies.

dixie_kong._note: Dixie Kong is the new character introduced in DKC2. Her helicopter spin is the defining new mechanic.

walk_speed.notes: Similar to Diddy — slightly slower or same. VERIFY.

jump.notes: Dixie's standard jump is slightly lower than Diddy's. Compensated by helicopter spin.

helicopter_spin.description: While airborne, pressing jump or holding jump causes Dixie to spin her ponytail like a helicopter, slowing descent and allowing horizontal travel.
helicopter_spin.activation: Press or hold jump button while airborne
helicopter_spin.effect_on_descent: Dramatically reduces downward velocity — near-horizontal glide
helicopter_spin.horizontal_speed_maintained: Maintains horizontal momentum from before spin activation
helicopter_spin.duration: Can be maintained for 3-4 seconds before Dixie 'runs out' and falls normally
notes: Helicopter spin allows crossing gaps that would require precise jump timing to navigate without it.
When carrying Diddy (Diddy on Dixie's back), both benefit from the helicopter spin.
The spin can be used mid-jump to extend horizontal distance significantly.
Level design in DKC2 has many gaps explicitly requiring helicopter spin for standard traversal.
Exact descent speed and horizontal speed during spin need ROM verification.

ponytail_attack.description: Dixie can attack with her ponytail (whip-like) when pressing B while not in run.
ponytail_attack.notes: Short-range melee attack. Primarily used for hitting enemies at close range.

tag_team.notes: Same 2-hit system as DKC1. First hit loses one partner; second (with remaining partner alone) = death.
tag_team.barrel_rescue: Diddy or Dixie barrels appear in stages to rescue the lost partner.

animal_buddies._note: Different set from DKC1. Highlights the new mechanics unique to DKC2.

squawks.description: Parrot. RIDEABLE in DKC2 (upgraded from lantern-carrier in DKC1). Can fly freely and attack by spitting nuts.
squawks.notes: Squawks sections are free-flight challenges. Can attack enemies by pressing fire button.

rattly.description: Rattlesnake. EXTREMELY high jump — higher than any Kong or other buddy.
rattly.notes: Rattly's super-jump is the highest vertical reach in the game. Sections using Rattly have tall vertical structures.

squitter.description: Spider. Creates web platforms by pressing fire. Web platforms can be jumped on.
squitter.notes: Squitter sections are puzzle-platforms: create webs to reach inaccessible areas. Web creation is the primary mechanic.

clapper.description: Seal. Claps to temporarily freeze hot lava into solid ground, or cool boiling water.
clapper.notes: Environmental interaction buddy. Creates temporary platforms in hazardous terrain.

rambi.description: Rhino. Returns from DKC1. Same high-speed charge mechanics.

glimmer.description: Anglerfish. Provides light in dark underwater sections.
glimmer.notes: Like DKC1 Squawks, purely a visibility mechanic. Not combat or traversal.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: Dixie's helicopter spin is the most mechanically impactful addition over DKC1. It turns large horizontal gaps from skill challenges into accessibility ramps — even if you don't time the jump perfectly, the spin can compensate. This shifts difficulty from 'can you clear this gap' to 'how efficiently can you navigate with the spin.'

DKC2's animal buddies are more mechanically varied than DKC1's. Squitter (web platforms), Clapper (terrain transformation), Rattly (extreme vertical reach) each create fundamentally different gameplay contexts within their sections.

Squitter's web-platform creation is the most puzzle-game-like mechanic in the DKC series. It requires spatial planning: place web here, jump to web, place next web ahead of it. This is light puzzle-game design within a platformer.

The helicopter spin changes the pace of DKC2 compared to DKC1. DKC1 requires more precise jumping; DKC2 allows more aerial improvisation. DKC2 is consequently considered more approachable while still deeply rewarding skilled play.

DKC2's level design is widely considered the high point of the trilogy — more varied, more inventive, and better paced than DKC1 and DKC3. The expanded mechanics (helicopter spin + new buddies) gave the designers more tools to work with.
